video:video.video:title: Pakistan's delegation arrives in Obbuergen for high-level talks between the United States and Iran
video:video.video:description: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and his delegation arrived in Switzerland on Sunday to participate in high-level talks between the United States and Iran on the implementation of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding being held in Switzerland.

video:video.video:title: Indian PM Modi leads Yoga day celebrations in Kolkata
video:video.video:description: People across India marked the International day of Yoga on Saturday with gatherings at public places for mass demonstrations of traditional yoga practice.

video:video.video:title: UK produces first extra virgin olive oil as farmers race to adapt to changing climate
video:video.video:description: The milestone comes as climate change disrupts traditional olive-growing regions across the Mediterranean, driving up prices as demand for premium olive oil continues to grow.

video:video.video:title: U.S. and Iranian officials arrive in Switzerland to launch talks on Tehran’s nuclear program
video:video.video:description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance and senior Iranian officials arrived in Switzerland on Sunday to formally launch negotiations over Tehran’s nuclear program, build out the fragile interim deal to end the war in Iran and keep the Strait of Hormuz open.

video:video.video:title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding stokes speculation in Rhode Island
video:video.video:description: Rumors that Taylor Swift was planning to marry at her Rhode Island home this weekend have drawn curious visitors to the seaside village of Watch Hill.

video:video.video:title: Welcome to the tiny South African vineyards creating special wines
video:video.video:description: South Africa’s Western Cape region has a winemaking tradition going back to the 17th century. But the most unique vineyards are often the smallest, creating small batches of niche wines.

video:video.video:title: Czechs rally against government plan to overhaul funding of public broadcasters
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Czechs rallied in the capital on Sunday to condemn a plan by the government led by populist Prime Minister Andrej Babiš to overhaul funding for public broadcasters that is considered dangerous for their independence.

video:video.video:title: Drought, wind and heat fuel wildfires in U.S. west and force evacuations
video:video.video:description: The Iron Fire in Utah’s Juab County was first detected Saturday and had blackened 34 square miles, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 21
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Sunday, June 21st: Vance meets Iran in Switzerland over interim war deal; Europe hit by heat wave; Colombia votes between de la Espriella and Cepeda; Kyiv Pride draws hundreds.

video:video.video:title: Parts of Europe swelter as heatwave continues
video:video.video:description: Italy, Spain, France and the United Kingdom swelters on Sunday as the heatwave intensifies and temperatures climb up to 40 Celsius.

video:video.video:title: Fans of Spain and soccer fill the streets of Atlanta before World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans have taken to the streets of Atlanta, throwing a raucous rolling party in support of Spain's soccer team.

video:video.video:title: Colombian presidential candidates vote in runoff round results
video:video.video:description: Colombian presidential contenders voted in a closely fought presidential runoff Sunday between a progressive and a conservative outsider.

video:video.video:title: 3 killed in small plane crash outside of DC suburb
video:video.video:description: A small plane crashed in the woods outside of a Washington, DC suburb late Saturday evening, killing its pilot and two passengers, police said.

video:video.video:title: Spain fans celebrate their World Cup win against Saudi Arabia
video:video.video:description: After a 4-0 win against Saudi Arabia, Spain fans celebrated their victory in front of Atlanta Stadium.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes leave widespread damage in Nabatiyeh as ceasefire appears to be holding in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Israeli strikes left widespread damage in Nabatiyeh in southern Lebanon as a ceasefire appeared to be holding on Sunday. (Production by Ali Sharafeddine)

video:video.video:title: Four capybaras born in Jerusalem’s Biblical Zoo named after the Beatles
video:video.video:description: Four Capybara pups born in Jerusalem’s Biblical Zoo were named after the Beatles, namely John, Paul, George and Ringo. (AP video: Shlomo Mor)

video:video.video:title: Uncertain progress as US Vice President Vance meets top Iranian officials in Switzerland
video:video.video:description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Sunday there was an opportunity to “turn over a new leaf” with Iran as the sides held talks aimed at building out the interim deal to end the war in Iran reached by the two sides last week.

video:video.video:title: Economic woes fuel discontent with Brexit in UK, decade after vote to leave EU
video:video.video:description: Some business leaders express frustration and discontent a decade after Britain voted to leave the European Union in 2016.

video:video.video:title: Football players in Gaza return to training, trying to make up for lost time during the war
video:video.video:description: On a modest soccer field in Gaza City surrounded by hollowed out buildings and rubble, Palestinian soccer players gathered for their training session, as the war had destroyed most of the fields where they used to play.

video:video.video:title: Reactions as Israeli strikes leave Lebanon's ancient coastal city of Tyre shaken
video:video.video:description: The dust has barely settled in Tyre after weeks of Israeli airstrikes on the ancient city along Lebanon's Mediterranean coast.

video:video.video:title: JD Vance speaks at the opening of US-Iran talks in Switzerland
video:video.video:description: U.S. and Iranian negotiators were in Switzerland on Sunday for talks on their interim agreement to end the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes leave Lebanon's ancient coastal city of Tyre shaken
video:video.video:description: The dust has barely settled in Tyre after weeks of Israeli airstrikes on the ancient city along Lebanon's Mediterranean coast.

video:video.video:title: Vance and Iranian officials arrive in Switzerland to launch talks on Tehran’s nuclear program
video:video.video:description: A team led by U.S. Vice President JD Vance is to meet with Iranian negotiators led by parliamentary Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi for talks on Tehran's nuclear program as part of a fragile deal to end the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Police announce biggest cocaine seizure recorded in Australia
video:video.video:description: Australian police said cocaine found on a property near Sydney is the nation’s "largest ever seizure" of the drug.

video:video.video:title: Prada goes back to basics
video:video.video:description: Prada serves up luxury fashion's version of pasta pomodoro at Milan Fashion Week, in a return to basics.

video:video.video:title: Prada goes back to basics
video:video.video:description: Prada serves up fashion's version of pasta pomodoro at Milan Fashion Week, a return to basics with a very unadorned, distilled collection. (June 21)

video:video.video:title: Jubilation in the diaspora as Cape Verde scores 1st World Cup goal and holds Uruguay to 2-2 draw
video:video.video:description: Members of one of the largest Cape Verdeans communities in the United States danced, cheered and rallied on the streets after their soccer team scored its first World Cup goal and held Uruguay to a 2-2 tie.

video:video.video:title: Scuffles break out in Inglewood after Iran vs Belgium World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Scuffles break out on the street as Iran plays Belgium in the World Cup

video:video.video:title: Taiwan military launches five-day combat readiness drills to boost rapid response capability
video:video.video:description: Taiwan’s military launched a five-day combat readiness exercise on Monday aimed at enhancing the armed forces’ ability to respond immediately to potential threats from China.

video:video.video:title: Keir Starmer announces his resignation as Prime Minister and Leader of the Labour Party
video:video.video:description: Keir Starmer says he'll step down as UK Labour Party leader, will remain UK prime minister until successor chosen. (AP video by Henry Wilkins)

video:video.video:title: Artist paints large Lionel Messi mural in Dallas
video:video.video:description: Artist Jose May, aka Joe Skilz, put the finishing touches on a large mural featuring Argentina star Lionel Messi in the Deep Ellum entertainment district in Dallas over the weekend.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 22
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, June 22nd: U.S.-Iran talks continue in Switzerland; Mediators report progress on several issues; Wildfires in western U.S.; Norway World Cup soccer fans “row” in New York’s Times Square.

video:video.video:title: Uncertain progress as Vance meets top Iranian officials in Switzerland
video:video.video:description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Sunday there was an opportunity to “turn over a new leaf” with Iran as the sides held talks aimed at building out the interim deal to end the war in Iran reached by the two sides last week.

video:video.video:title: Norway fans perform 'Viking row' in Times Square before World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Norwegian football fans converge at New York's iconic Times Square ahead of their second Group I clash against Senegal, which is set for Monday night.

video:video.video:title: No maps, no insurance: Michigan floods expose lack of information, preparation in many rural areas
video:video.video:description: Many homeowners hit by devastating Michigan floods had no insurance and no idea they were at risk — underscoring vulnerabilities throughout rural America as climate change causes more extreme weather.

video:video.video:title: Keir Starmer announces he’ll resign as UK prime minister
video:video.video:description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Monday he is stepping down as leader of the governing Labour Party and will leave office within weeks, scarcely two years after being elected in a landslide.

video:video.video:title: Keir Starmer announces resignation as UK prime minister and Labour Party leader
video:video.video:description: Keir Starmer says he'll step down as UK Labour Party leader, will remain UK prime minister until successor chosen.

video:video.video:title: Fans celebrate in Vancouver as Egypt tops New Zealand 3-1 in World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Fans of Egypt’s World Cup team celebrated in Vancouver, British Columbia, on Sunday as Egypt defeated New Zealand 3-1.

video:video.video:title: S3 of 'House of the Dragon' takes off
video:video.video:description: Stars promise the biggest and most epic season yet as "House of the Dragon" returns for season 3.

video:video.video:title: Keir Starmer's rise to power and resignation as UK prime minister
video:video.video:description: Keir Starmer said Monday he'll step down as UK Labour Party leader, will remain UK prime minister until successor chosen.

video:video.video:title: Who is Andy Burnham, the lawmaker seeking to replace Keir Starmer?
video:video.video:description: The 56-year-old politician presents himself as an amiable northern everyman who prefers T-shirts to a suit and tie and spends spare time playing soccer or spinning 1990s tunes during DJ battles.

video:video.video:title: Milan Fashion Week - menswear trends
video:video.video:description: In complicated times, Milan designers went lighter — if not in materials, then in silhouette. Amid economic uncertainty, geopolitical tensions and a sweltering Milan Fashion Week, designers largely stripped things back for next summer, embracing clean lines and pared-down looks.

video:video.video:title: Russia strikes civilian infrastructure in various parts of Ukraine
video:video.video:description: At least one person was killed and three others injured from a Russian missile strike on civilian infrastructure in Ukraine’s Odesa region, local officials said on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Students cry and hug each other after school shooting in the Philippines
video:video.video:description: Two young students opened fire in a high school in the central Philippines on Monday, killing three fellow students and wounding another seven, police said.

video:video.video:title: Starmer says he'll resign as UK prime minister with Burnham confirming he will try to succeed him
video:video.video:description: Starmer says he'll resign as UK prime minister with Burnham confirming he will try to succeed him

video:video.video:title: Starmer challenger Andy Burnham sworn in as UK lawmaker
video:video.video:description: Andy Burnham, who won a special parliamentary election last week, was sworn in as a lawmaker in the UK Parliament on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Former Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan dies at 100
video:video.video:description: Alan Greenspan, the jazz-playing U.S. Federal Reserve chair who was celebrated for engineering a decade of prosperity but later shared the blame for a devastating financial crisis, died Monday.

video:video.video:title: US and Iran wrap up initial round of peace talks in Switzerland, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance on Monday said his lengthy talks with senior Iranian officials in Switzerland created a “good foundation for a successful final deal” as they seek a permanent end to the war that the U.S. and Israel began in late February.

video:video.video:title: Vance says talks in Switzerland with Iranian officials set 'good foundation' for deal to end war
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance said Monday peace talks with Iran created a “good foundation for a successful final deal” to end the war that began at the end of February.

video:video.video:title: Starmer challenger Andy Burnham sworn in as UK lawmaker
video:video.video:description: Starmer challenger Andy Burnham sworn in as UK lawmaker

video:video.video:title: Japanese royal family visits Belgian royal family at the Castle of Laeken
video:video.video:description: The Emperor and Empress of Japan were welcomed by the Belgian royal family at the Royal Castle of Laeken in Brussels on Monday. (AP Video by Mark Carlson)

video:video.video:title: Family of infant shot by Mississippi police seeks independent autopsy
video:video.video:description: The family of a 1-year-old boy killed by police in Mississippi who were responding to a shoplifting report is calling on authorities to release video of the shooting.

video:video.video:title: Tom Holland and Zendaya swing into Berlin
video:video.video:description: Tom Holland and Zendaya met hundreds of fans in Berlin on Monday ahead of the launch of their upcoming movie “Spider-Man: Brand New Day.” (June 22)

video:video.video:title: Clive Davis, music industry starmaker, has died at 94
video:video.video:description: Music industry titan Clive Davis— who started as a record company lawyer and went on to launch the careers of superstars like Janis Joplin, Bruce Springsteen, Barry Manilow, Whitney Houston, and Alicia Keys— has died at 94.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 22
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, June 22nd: Starmer resigns as UK's Labour leader; Fire tears through commercial building in northern India; Parisians ands tourists turn to museums as temperatures soar in France; Belgian royals greet emperor and empress of Japan.

video:video.video:title: Vance leaves Switzerland, but other US negotiators are still talking with Iran
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance said his lengthy talks with senior Iranian officials in Switzerland created a good foundation for a final deal to end the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Why are pink cleats the World Cup’s hottest fashion trend?
video:video.video:description: From Cristiano Ronaldo to Kylian Mbappé, many of soccer’s biggest stars are wearing pink cleats at the World Cup. Here’s why.

video:video.video:title: Montreal shooting leaves one officer dead, another injured
video:video.video:description: A Montreal police officer is dead and another officer is injured following a shooting that prompted a major police response and multiple crime scenes Monday in the Côte-des-Neiges neighborhood, according to CTV News.

video:video.video:title: Lionel Messi becomes top scorer in World Cup history
video:video.video:description: Scoring goals and breaking records is what Lionel Messi does. The Argentina captain, who many consider the greatest player of all time, scored two more World Cup goals on Monday in his team’s 2-0 victory over Austria.

video:video.video:title: 'The name Clive Davis carries so much weight,' says Rolling Stone co-editor-in-chief
video:video.video:description: Rolling Stone co-editor-in-chief Shirley Halperin says Clive Davis was "responsible for so much of the soundtrack to our lives" and leaves a monumental legacy. (June 22)

video:video.video:title: Colombians react to runoff election as Trump-endorsed de la Espriella holds slim lead
video:video.video:description: Colombians reflected on the country's polarized presidential runoff in which conservative political outsider Abelardo de la Espriella held a narrow lead on Monday, with almost all votes counted.

video:video.video:title: Black jaguar at Rio’s BioParque makes World Cup prediction for Brazil vs Scotland
video:video.video:description: Black jaguar at Rio’s BioParque makes World Cup prediction for Brazil vs Scotland

video:video.video:title: World Cup fans seek photos and some luck at Philadelphia's Rocky statue
video:video.video:description: Thousands of World Cup fans visit Philadelphia's Rocky statue for photos each day. Some locals believe the statue brings bad luck to any team whose jersey is placed on it.

video:video.video:title: Conservation group donates equipment to help prevent human-gorilla transmission of Ebola
video:video.video:description: As Uganda battles a deadly outbreak of the Ebola virus, conservationists are increasing efforts to protect the country's mountain gorillas.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Milly Alcock on battling the 'fear' of taking on 'Supergirl' role
video:video.video:description: Milly Alcock stars as Kara Zor-El, better known as "Supergirl", in the latest installment of James Gunn's expanding DC Universe. (June 22)

video:video.video:title: Trump praises Starmer but says he was hurt by immigration and windmills
video:video.video:description: The president was asked about the British Prime Minister Keir Starmer and cited one of his longstanding grievances with wind energy policy.

video:video.video:title: Trump warns Iran: ‘I’ll do what I have to do’ if deal is violated
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump warned Monday, "I will do what I have to do” if Iran fails to uphold it's peace deal with Washington.

video:video.video:title: James Gunn on Superman: 'What if it was real?'
video:video.video:description: James Gunn explains the storyline behind Clark Kent's hypno-glasses in latest DCU film, "Supergirl."

video:video.video:title: Heat stress growing because of prolonged heatwaves in many nations, say researchers
video:video.video:description: Heatwaves around the world are getting hotter and lasting longer according to a new study. Scientists analyzed a global dataset of human heat stress using a measurement called the Universal Thermal Climate Index and found some subtropical countries are experiencing 50 more days per year of at least

video:video.video:title: 'They went in there with a knife:' Trump claims 'vandals' damaged reflecting pool
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said multiple arrests have been made and claimed authorities have "proof" to support the "vandalism" allegations.

video:video.video:title: Iran’s lead negotiator insists Strait of Hormuz will be managed by Iran
video:video.video:description: The lead negotiator of the Iranian delegation, Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf, insisted on Monday the Strait of Hormuz will be managed by Iran and would follow international laws.

video:video.video:title: A look at the quick succession of British prime ministers in the past 10 years
video:video.video:description: Britain is about to get its seventh prime minister in a single decade. On Monday, Keir Starmer announced his resignation, forced out by his own party after just two years and marking the sixth time a leader has given a farewell speech outside 10 Downing Street since 2016.

video:video.video:title: Argentina fans react after Messi sets World Cup scoring record
video:video.video:description: Lionel Messi set a World Cup record with his 17th and 18th goals, and defending champion Argentina advanced to the knockout stage with a 2-0 victory over Austria on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Ella Purnell on being a part of the 'Fallout,' 'Yellowjackets' and 'Arcane' fandoms
video:video.video:description: Ella Purnell says it's incredible to witness the fandoms from popular series like "Fallout," "Yellowjackets" and "Arcane" come together. (June 22)

video:video.video:title: Priced out of World Cup games, Mexican fans take celebrations into their own hands
video:video.video:description: Priced out of World Cup games, Mexican fans take celebrations into their own hands

video:video.video:title: Shooting at a Northern California library kills 2, a suspect is in custody
video:video.video:description: A shooting at a library in Northern California on Monday left two people dead and a suspect in custody, according to police.

video:video.video:title: France sizzles in punishing heat that's already causing deaths
video:video.video:description: Parisians and tourists tried to beat the heat on Monday by seeking shade and flocking to air conditioned museums, as temperatures in the French capital reached 44 degrees Celsius (111.2 Fahrenheit).

video:video.video:title: Colombia players greet fans in Mexico's Guadalajara ahead of World Cup match against DRCongo
video:video.video:description: Colombia supporters gathered Monday outside the national team's hotel in Mexico, taking part in what Colombians commonly call a "banderazo" ahead of the team's World Cup group-stage match against Congo.

video:video.video:title: North Korean leader Kim emphasizes nuclear weapons need during Workers' Party meeting
video:video.video:description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has stressed his need for nuclear weapons at a meeting of his ruling Workers' Party, state media reported early Tuesday.
